Ready to start. Choose the combination that serves you today: human calendar reminder, guided prompt in Cowork, or recurring AI facilitator once the habit is proven. They are not mutually exclusive paths. Start with DBR, Daily Close, and WBR; when you have evidence of use, convert the stable cadence into a supervised scheduled task so the AI asks, summarizes, proposes, and waits for your confirmation before logging.

Mov. V · Closing · §21 / 23

§21Footnotes · acronyms and technical terms

This section defines the acronyms and technical terms that appear in the body of the document, in simple language. The intention is for the document to be read smoothly without an acronym slowing you down.

Note 1 · Personal operating system
The operating system is the base software of your computer (Windows, macOS, Linux). Here we use the metaphor because a well-configured Cowork functions as a base layer on which you install tools, define routines, and delegate work.
Note 2 · Tool-level map
Twelve floors grouped into three movements. Movement one (solve with what's already there): Direct Chat, Chat with MCP connectors, Chat Projects. Movement two (work with what you have): Direct Cowork, Cowork Projects. Movement three (build what you need): Skills, Plugins, mini-apps via vibe coding, versioned plugin engineering, Project station, mini-apps deployed for the browser. NotebookLM is integrated as the first MCP connector for Cowork.
Note 3 · Station
A station in this system is a folder dedicated to an area of your work, with its own instruction file, its own memory file, and its own reference files. In operational terms, it is a stable area within your virtual office map: each station is a daily work domain (Mail, Delivery, Pre-sales) that occupies its Base sector in the system.
Note 4 · NotebookLM
A Google tool where you upload sources (PDFs, links, transcripts) and ask it questions, which the system answers by citing exactly which source each statement comes from. It is useful when the traceability of the citation matters more than speed.
Note 5 · Claude Projects via Chat
Focused thematic spaces that any user can create from claude.ai with their own instructions and uploaded reference files. The difference with Cowork Projects is that Chat projects live in the cloud and do not touch local files.
Note 6 · Claude
Claude is the name of the AI model made by Anthropic that operates in both Chat and Cowork.
Note 7 · MCP
MCP stands for Model Context Protocol. It is the technical standard that defines how Cowork connects to external applications like Gmail, Calendar, or Notion. The metaphor is that of a USB cable.
Note 8 · Vibe coding
A colloquial way of programming where the human describes what they want in natural language and the AI writes the code. The virtue is prototyping speed; the risk is fragility in the face of environmental changes.
Note 9 · ADR
ADR stands for Architecture Decision Record. It is a short, one-page note that documents a decision made, the alternatives considered, the expected consequences, and the date.
Note 10 · PMO
PMO stands for Project Management Office. It is the area within a company that defines methodology, templates, metrics, and common practices.
Note 11 · CI/CD
CI/CD stands for Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment. It is the practice of automating software testing and deployment.
Note 12 · SLA
SLA stands for Service Level Agreement. It is the explicit commitment regarding the availability, response time, and quality of a service.
Note 13 · Guardrails
Guardrails literally translates to protection rails. In AI, they are the explicit restrictions that limit what a model or an agent can do.
Note 14 · Evaluation harness
In AI, an evaluation harness is the set of tests and metrics that automatically assess whether a model, plugin, or agent is working well.
Note 15 · Deep research
Deep research is the practice of in-depth, AI-assisted investigation where the system searches multiple sources (a minimum of four is recommended), cross-references them, and produces a brief with traceable citations.
Mov. V · Closing · §22 / 23

§22Risks, limits, and validation

Three areas require active validation. Numerical data for Anthropic products (exact window, quotas, connectors per plan, prompt cache) are labeled as assumptions · verify in official documentation [11] before making plan or investment decisions. Cited public content was extracted from verified descriptions and indexes, not complete transcripts · watch the videos directly for operational details. Pace of change of the Cowork product is high · review this playbook semi-annually.

Sensitive domain: in crisis communications, formal feedback, contractual decisions, or matters with legal, labor, or reputational consequences, validate with Legal, People, or Compliance. The AI is an assistant, not a legal or human resources advisor. Recurring emails and communications require sustained human supervision for the first few weeks.

MetodologIA deontological filter · hard gates before publishing

Every piece produced with Jarvis's assistance (email, status report, proposal, documented decision, communication to steering) must pass the four questions before going out into the world. If any answer is no, the piece is not published.

  1. Method · is there a diagnosis before the assertion, or am I applying a tool without understanding the flow?
  2. Sovereignty · is the reader more autonomous after reading this, or more dependent on me or the system?
  3. Dignity · am I glorifying hustle, permanent sprinting, always-on, or am I treating the reader as an adult who deserves time and attention?
  4. Evidence · Is there a number with context and a verifiable source, or am I just throwing out adjectives without measurement?

If all four pass, you publish. If one fails, you rewrite. The playbook applies this same filter to itself; any section that doesn't pass it must be flagged in the open feedback of §19.5 to be rewritten in the next wave.

Mov. V · Closing · §23 / 23 — last section

Mov. V Close · Document Closure You've reached the end. → What follows is outside the playbook: open Cowork, seed your CLAUDE.md, and begin your full competency Q. What I build, I share.
A

Appendix · Author's Cases

What follows are specific decisions the author made in their repo during the May 2026 elevation. They are cited as a reference to inspire the reader's own solution, not as a canon to be replicated. The criterion for something to live in this appendix and not in the body of the playbook is clear: if it assumes the author's specific configuration, it lives here; if it is replicable by any reader with a functional Jarvis, it lives in the body.

A.1 · Multi-agent Stack · AGENTS.md as a mirror

The author operates a multi-agent stack in the same repo: Claude (Cowork, Claude Code, Claude Desktop) coexists with Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and other agents that follow the convention of AGENTS.md (reference: github.com/agents-md). To ensure all agents read the same canon without having to maintain two files by hand, AGENTS.md is regenerated mirror of CLAUDE.md via a script (scripts/sync-agents-md.sh) that runs after each structural edit of CLAUDE.md. The rule is: the canon is CLAUDE.md, the mirror is AGENTS.md, never the other way around.

Why it is NOT inherited into the canon. The pattern assumes an active multi-agent stack. The typical reader operates with a single agent. Forcing the pattern adds complexity without being useful. If the reader eventually operates multi-agent, this appendix serves as a reference to inspire their own solution. Full pattern detail: ADR memory/decisiones/20260513_root_agents-md-multi-agent-paralelo.md in the author's repo.

A.2 · Productivity plugin ecosystem · temporary snapshot

Current list at the time of writing this appendix (May 2026). This is not a recommendation—the reader should verify availability in their Claude/Cowork client at the time of adoption, and choose the tool their team uses or that fits their visual workflow. The productivity ecosystem changes approximately every 18 months; any nominal list ages quickly. Keeping the choice abstract in the body of the playbook protects the reader from adopting a deprecated tool or avoiding a new one that did not exist when the guide was written.

Snapshot May 2026. In the productivity and tasks category: Linear, ClickUp, Asana, Monday, Notion. In the enterprise-search and documents category: Atlassian (Jira/Confluence), Guru, MS365 (Outlook/SharePoint/Teams), Slack. The sync convention (canonical markdown, mirror plugin, suffix [PROVIDER-ID] inline, manual sync in WBR) is the same for all. The difference is how well each one fits the reader's workflow.

A.3 · IIKit as Jarvis infrastructure

The author adopted Intent Integrity Kit (IIKit) as the conceptual infrastructure for Jarvis during May 2026. The five IIKit rules live as a module in 00_Recursos/iikit-reglas-adoptadas/ at a philosophical level, and the operational implementation was piloted in the project Runbook OS (P-006) using a vendored tile via Tessl. Adoption into the author's other projects is gradual and will be evaluated in QBR Q3 2026 with real metrics.

Why it is NOT inherited into the canon. Author's personal decision based on specific validation of their context. The reader evaluates for themselves whether IIKit or another phase discipline framework is useful. Full detail: ADR memory/decisiones/20260511_root_adopcion-iikit-dos-planos.md.

A.4 · Pristino · lineage of the author's own agents

The author maintains an umbrella brand ("Pristino") for their own agents with six slots and differentiated licensing per incarnation. The slots are: Pristino-NPC (historical role origin), Pristino-Brand (previous brands), Pristino-Venture (failed venture turned into a lesson), Pristino-MOAT (OSS technical pattern), Pristino-Jarvis (this Cowork assistant), and a reserved slot for future incarnations. Each slot has its own architecture and license without contaminating the others.

Why it is NOT inherited into the canon. Author's personal branding decision. A reader building their own lineage of agents will see this as inspiration, not an obligation. Full detail: 00_Recursos/marca-personal/pristino-linaje.md and ADR memory/decisiones/20260511_root_pristino-linaje.md.

The operational criterion for writing a new appendix: if the decision assumes the author's configuration, it goes here; if it is replicable by any reader with a functional Jarvis, it goes in the body of the playbook. The difference matters because the inheritable canon must be executable without the author's context.

MetodologIA Co-creating environments of abundance

Authorship · MetodologIA

Enablers of disruption. Co-creator of abundance environments for people and organizations to unlock digital and professional sovereignty. Author of the Playbook · Personal Jarvis OS · contributor to the MetodologIA playbook Learn · Apprehend · (R)Evolve [12].

MetodologIA Team · four strategists, four pillars

MetodologIA is not built alone. The collective is supported by four operational pillars · each co-founder leads one. Empowerment designs the systems that turn potential into results. Efficiency eliminates the unnecessary and multiplies what moves the needle. Ecosystem connects talent and purpose in living alliances. Enablement designs frictionless environments where people shine.

MetodologIA · metodologia.info · CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (Copyleft) · What I build, I share

CAP 01 · Local File Access

What it is, exactly

A filesystem sandbox on your computer where Cowork can read, write, create, move, and edit files without you having to paste them in every conversation. Authorization is explicit per folder and can be revoked at any time.

Why it matters

It eliminates the most costly friction in daily work with AI: pasting documents one by one in each turn. A client folder with twelve previous status reports, archived meeting minutes, the current plan, and the risk register remains permanently available. The estimated savings are between fifteen and thirty minutes per productive session.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Open Cowork for the first time.
  2. Create a dedicated folder in Documents named 'Trabajo en Claude Desktop'.
  3. Inside, create subfolders by station and by client.
  4. In Cowork, click the folder icon and authorize 'Trabajo en Claude Desktop' as the working directory.
  5. Star the folder so it loads by default in each new session.
  6. Validate with a test file: upload a PDF, ask 'read this PDF and give me a summary in three bullets'.

Use cases

Permanently accessible client folder · processing receipt photos into Excel · splitting large PDFs · converting legacy PowerPoint files · analyzing CSVs without pasting content.

Antipatterns to avoid

Authorizing the entire home directory instead of a dedicated folder. Mixing personal and corporate folders in the same working directory. Allowing read access to folders with personally identifiable information without prior filtering.

CAP 02 · Persistent Memory

What it is, exactly

The ability for Cowork to read and write plain text files (CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md) that persist between sessions. CLAUDE.md is the instruction manual that Cowork reads at the beginning of each conversation. MEMORY.md is the notebook where learnings and preferences are accumulated.

Why it matters

Without persistent memory, every conversation starts from scratch. With persistent memory, Cowork learns with you and performs better over time instead of remaining static. The cumulative savings are exponential: each learning saves time in hundreds of future conversations.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Activate persistent memory in the Cowork configuration panel.
  2. Create the root CLAUDE.md file with three critical sections: Memory System, Routing map, References.
  3. Create the root MEMORY.md file with two sections: Memory and Active Projects.
  4. Seed the initial profile by telling Cowork 'I want you to remember the following about me'.
  5. Validate with 'show me what you remember about me' and review the resulting list.

Use cases

Voice principles for Cowork to write documents in your natural voice. Client's stakeholder map. Legacy naming conventions. Prudence rules for critical communications. Status of active projects.

Antipatterns to avoid

Memory without a monthly audit. An inflated CLAUDE.md with more than three hundred lines. Repeating the same rules in multiple files. Seeding the memory with frequently changing data.

CAP 03 · MCP Connectors

What it is, exactly

MCP (Model Context Protocol) integrations that give Cowork the ability to read and write to external applications like Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Notion, Jira, Asana, or Slack. Each connector requires explicit authorization with scope and available revocation.

Why it matters

Without connectors, Cowork only sees what is in your local folder. With connectors, cross-tool workflows cease to be manual work and become queries that Cowork executes in seconds.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Open Customize in Cowork.
  2. Go to the Connectors section.
  3. Always start with Gmail and Calendar.
  4. Authorize with the minimum necessary scope.
  5. Validate with a test prompt.
  6. Operate for two weeks with real use before adding a third connector.

Use cases

Morning inbox triage with automatic classification. Summary of the day's agenda. Cross-reference between transcription and notes. Risk register synchronization. Semantic search in knowledge bases.

Antipatterns to avoid

Activating ten connectors on the first day. Granting broad scopes 'just in case'. Keeping authorized connectors unused for more than sixty days. Not revoking access when a person leaves the team.

CAP 04 · Skills

What it is, exactly

Reusable packages of instructions, templates, and optionally scripts that Cowork loads on-demand when it detects the task needs them. They are installed via Customize, Skills, Upload a Skill, and are invoked by name or by automatically detected context.

Why it matters

Without skills, repetitive tasks are executed manually each time. With skills, a short conversation produces the same consistent deliverable that used to take half an hour. Rule of three: if a pattern repeats three or more times with the same structure, it's worth materializing it.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Enable the Anthropic skill-creator in Customize, Skills.
  2. Operate the manual flow with three real examples.
  3. Give iterative feedback on each output until it is exactly what you want.
  4. Ask Cowork "now go back over our conversation and create a skill that captures this flow".
  5. Validate the skill by running it against a fourth, different example.
  6. Upload the skill via Customize.

Use cases

Status report skill with the client's template. Meeting minutes skill with the adopted structure. Risk entry skill with mandatory fields. Retrospective skill with the team's format.

Antipatterns to avoid

Creating skills from scratch without operating the manual flow first. Not backing up skills on Drive. Promoting a skill to official without validating it for two weeks in real use. Having skills with overlapping responsibilities.

CAP 05 · Cowork Projects

What it is, exactly

Cowork containers that group custom instructions, reference files, conversations, and persistent memory under a specific domain. Each Project has its own declared role, conventions, and history that persists on the filesystem.

Why it matters

Without Projects, all knowledge work is mixed into a single flow where Cowork has to guess the context. With Projects, each client has a coherent context that reproduces voice and format effortlessly.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Create a new Project with the client's or initiative's name.
  2. Upload five to ten curated reference files.
  3. Write custom instructions with four elements: tone, format, naming, prudence.
  4. Set the default model for the Project.
  5. Have a test conversation asking for a draft.
  6. Seed MEMORY.md with key stakeholders.

Use cases

A Project for each active client with all critical documents uploaded. A Project for Pre-sales with proposal templates and success stories. A Project for the Toolbox. A Project for Coaching/People. A Project for Comms.

Antipatterns to avoid

A Project with thirty unfiltered files. Vague custom instructions. Mixing two clients into a single Project. Keeping Projects unused for more than sixty days.

CAP 06 · Browser Extension

What it is, exactly

A Chrome extension that gives Cowork agentic capabilities over web pages. It allows Cowork to click buttons, fill out forms, navigate between tabs, and operate web interfaces under human supervision.

Why it matters

There are web tasks that do not have an API or their own MCP, such as internal dashboards, client portals, or regulatory forms. The extension allows operating them with AI assistance. The public source documents important limitations.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Install the Claude extension from the Chrome Web Store.
  2. Configure the page scope, authorizing only work domains.
  3. Set the capture mode to manual selection initially.
  4. Keep cross-page persistence disabled unless explicitly needed.
  5. Validate with a simple test task.
  6. Audit usage weekly during the first month.

Use cases

Initiate returns on e-commerce portals. Complete regulatory web forms. Capture information from internal dashboards without an API. Operate supplier portals without native integration.

Antipatterns to avoid

Relying on the extension for critical flows until its maturity improves. Activating it in sessions with sensitive information exposed. Using it when a native MCP alternative is available.

CAP 07 · Scheduled Tasks

What it is, exactly

Cowork's ability to execute tasks at defined times and under specific conditions, without manual user triggering. It allows converting repetitive flows into automations that run while the user is sleeping or working on something else.

Why it matters

Without scheduled tasks, Cowork is a reactive assistant that only works when asked. With scheduled tasks, Cowork becomes a proactive assistant that prepares the groundwork for the day.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Define the task with five elements: exact cadence, trigger conditions, scope of actions, notification mode, backup plan.
  2. Configure the task in a trial mode with no side effects for two weeks.
  3. Review the output daily during those two weeks.
  4. Adjust prompts and rules based on findings.
  5. Promote to full automation only when the output is stable.
  6. Maintain a weekly audit of the output during the first quarter.

Use cases

Morning inbox triage with classification. Weekly pre-status draft on Thursdays. Automated weekly review summary on Fridays. QBR trigger every quarter. Risk register reminder every Monday.

Antipatterns to avoid

Configuring a scheduled task with a real sending scope from day one. Activating multiple tasks at once without having validated each one. Not reviewing the output daily during the first week. Keeping active tasks that produce output that no one reads anymore.

CAP 08 · Dispatch beta

What it is, exactly

A beta feature in Cowork for assigning tasks from a mobile device to a persistent conversation running on a computer with Claude Desktop open. It uses the files, connectors, plugins, and apps already configured and available on the desktop.

Why it matters

Reduce the loss between idea and execution. Instead of saving a note to process later, you capture the instruction when it appears and let Jarvis land it in the correct workspace.

How to set it up, step-by-step

  1. Update Claude Desktop and Claude mobile.
  2. Open Cowork and access Dispatch from the side panel.
  3. Complete the setup with file access only if you understand the scope.
  4. Test a harmless edit on a sandbox file.
  5. Validate where the output went and what notifications you received.
  6. Adopt it only for specific changes until you have operational confidence.

Use cases

Add an item to TAREAS.md from your mobile. Plant an improvement idea in a Project. Request a draft from local files before getting to your desk. Log fresh learnings at the end of a meeting away from the computer.

Antipatterns to avoid

Sending destructive tasks from mobile. Activating Dispatch without understanding which files and apps become available. Using it with sensitive data without an explicit policy. Assuming a feature that Anthropic keeps in beta is stable.

Floor 01 · Direct Chat · the first leap

Why this floor is the most important one to question

Floor 01 is where 90% of AI users get stuck. It's Direct Chat · one question, one answer, close the window, open another. It seems like the natural first step above the ground floor (not using AI), but it's exactly where the potential is diluted · because whoever always operates on floor 01 NEVER accumulates context, NEVER builds a Jarvis, NEVER capitalizes on learnings. The AI becomes an expensive search engine that forgets everything. The floor 01 trap is feeling productive (answers come quickly) while losing the real leverage · which is on floors 4 and 5 and up, where context is persisted.

When to YES use floor 01

A single, scoped question · validating an idea in three sentences before investing an hour exploring it · working on the go without access to your Jarvis · a pedagogical explanation to understand a concept without opening a Project. Typical usage time · < 5 minutes per session. Healthy frequency · 1-3 times a day as a supplement, NOT as the main tool.

When NOT to use floor 01

Any recurring task (status reports, email drafting, client management) does NOT go here · go directly up to floor 04 or 05. Any deep exploration of a topic (more than 4 turns) does NOT go here · go up to floor 03 (Chat Project) or 06 (Lab session). Any work with your files does NOT go here · only Cowork (floor 04+) sees them. If you catch yourself pasting the same thing twice in a row on floor 01 · that's the sign · go up a floor.

Explicit technical limitations

Context evaporates when the conversation is closed, unless you have memory activated according to your plan. It does NOT access your local computer files. It does NOT invoke MCP connectors (Gmail, Calendar, Drive). It does NOT write to your filesystem. It does NOT remember what you talked about yesterday unless the plan's memory allows it and you explicitly planted it.

Voice that activates this floor

"Quick question about X" · "validate this idea for me in three sentences" · "explain Y to me as if I were fifteen" · "just one thing · what if...?". If your question starts with "help me build" or "let's build together" · it's NOT floor 01 · take it up to the correct floor before investing time.

The leap to floor 02 · sign and technique

The sign to move up is repetition · when you notice you're mentally pasting the same prompt three times in a row, that's the transition to floor 02 (Chat with curated prompts). Building your repertoire of tested prompts is the first asset of your Jarvis · it exists even before you touch Cowork. The technique · every time a prompt gives you good output, save it in your own note with its context and the inputs it needed.

Floor 02 · Chat with high-performance prompts

What it is

It's still Chat, but you stop improvising prompts every turn. You build a personal library of curated prompts — tested templates that you reuse when a known task appears (summarizing minutes, breaking down problems, providing adversarial critique, drafting an executive email). Optionally, you enable MCP connectors when the prompt needs to look outside the chat (Gmail · Calendar · Drive).

When to move up

When you notice you're mentally pasting the same prompt three times in a row. That's the sign: it's worth formalizing it as a template and putting it in your personal prompt repository (Notion, Drive, snippets, text expander).

Difference from Chat Projects

Your prompts live in your own reusable repository, not locked inside a specific Project. You operate with a tested repertoire, not with turn-by-turn improvisation. This drastically reduces the cognitive friction of repetitive work in chat.

Floor 03 · Chat Projects

When to use

A focused thematic task that needs a stable thematic framework: pre-sales, committee preparation, written feedback for collaborators. It works like a personalized chatbot that maintains thematic context between conversations within the same project.

Configuration

Create from claude.ai with your own instructions and reference files uploaded to Anthropic's cloud. Lightweight and quick to configure.

Difference from Cowork Projects

They live in the cloud, they don't access the local filesystem. They are useful for repeated conceptual work but not for your client folder with twelve historical status reports.

Floor 04 · Direct Cowork

When to use

One-off productive sessions that touch your local files but don't yet deserve a formal Project: ad hoc analysis of a dataset, PDF to chapter (R)Evolution, legacy PowerPoint conversion.

Configuration

Open Cowork on an authorized working folder. Cowork can then read and write in that folder during the session.

When to upgrade to Cowork Projects

When you start repeating work with the same folder three times in a row, it's worth formalizing it as a Project with custom instructions.

Floor 05 · Cowork Projects

When to use

Recurring work with a specific client, initiative, or program. Each Project has its declared role, its tone and format conventions, its critical files, and its own memory that grows with each conversation.

Difference from Chat Projects

Cowork ones touch your local files, remember more, connect to more external tools, and persist on the filesystem as real folders.

Essential configuration

Custom instructions with tone, format, naming, and prudence rules. Five to ten curated reference files (no more). A Project-specific MEMORY.md.

Floor 06 · Skills

When to create one

Rule of three: if the pattern repeats three or more times with the same structure, it's worth materializing it as a skill.

How to build it well

Operate the manual flow first with three real examples, give iterative feedback until the output is exact, and then ask Cowork to extract the pattern into an installable skill.

Typical cases

Status report, meeting minutes, risk entry, retrospective, one-on-one coaching, lightweight ADR.

Floor 07 · Plugins

When to create one

When a pattern repeats across two or more knowledge workers on the same team. That's when the effort to package it as an installable plugin with versioning discipline is justified.

Difference from a skill

Where a skill serves one knowledge worker, a plugin serves several. Where a skill lives in your personal workspace, a plugin lives in a private or public store.

Minimum discipline

CHANGELOG, quality contract, an owner with maintenance responsibility, semantic versioning cycle (1.2.3).

Floor 08 · Mini-apps via vibe coding

What it is

A Hyperspecific App is a tiny, very specific mini-tool that solves a bottleneck that is yours and yours alone. Vibe coding means describing the intent in natural language and letting the AI write the code while you validate.

Typical cases

A mini-form that captures committee decisions and archives them in ADR format. A script that rotates retrospective templates among three formats. A tool that classifies risks from various projects according to a common schema.

Where to build it

Inside a Cowork Project that maintains the app's context as it grows and is refined.

Floor 09 · Versioned plugin engineering

When to move up to this level

When a mini-app is validated in real-world use and two or more knowledge workers on the team start using it. That's when it's worth promoting it to a formal plugin with engineering discipline.

Necessary discipline

Explicit quality contracts, unit tests for critical behavior, semantic versioning (1.2.3), scheduled retirement policies, integration with testing pipelines, peer review before promotion to official status.

When NOT to move up to this level

If the mini-app is used by only one person or changes significantly every week. Over-engineering is the most common cost at this level.

Floor 10 · Orchestrator station Project

What it is

A broader Project that no longer solves a single task but an entire area of work. It orchestrates various skills and plugins like a conductor, not like an executor of loose scripts.

Examples

Emails that combine the inbox triage skill with the personal voice drafts plugin and the morning summary scheduled task. Deliveries with the status report skill plus the risk entry plugin plus the meeting minutes skill. Pre-sales with RFP analysis plus proposals plus PERT estimation.

Floor 11 · Deployed web mini-apps

What it is

This level turns a personal mini-app into a web tool accessible from any browser, making it available to colleagues, clients, or roles that don't use Cowork directly.

How to build it

Inside a Project using vibe coding, you add a deployment layer (typically a simple web application hosting service), and the result is a URL that anyone can open.

Closing the loop

It moves from using a private tool to publishing an interface that others can use without knowing your setup. This multiplies the reach of your Jarvis from personal to communal.

Floor 12 · Porting YOUR context to another agentic tool · Digital Sovereignty

What it is

The twelfth floor is not a fifth mode of Claude Cowork. It is the portability test of YOUR context · not of the product. The ability to take your files (Root · Estaciones · Projects · Lab · Cadencias · custom skills · voice-principles · history) and reproduce the entire system on another agentic tool on the market in less than a Q. When your context survives without Cowork, you have reached the final step. Digital sovereign = my way of working travels with me.

What the migration looks like

  1. CLAUDE.md → the other platform's instructions. Your root file translates literally to the "system instructions" of Antigravity, the "custom instructions" of VS Code Copilot Workspace, or the equivalent in Codex.
  2. MEMORY.md → the rival's persistent memory. You dump the file into the new platform's memory API; if it doesn't have native memory, you keep MEMORY.md as a reference file that is loaded in each session.
  3. Skills → instructions in its language. Each Cowork skill has an equivalent: prompt template + file template. You rewrite the SKILL.md to its format (in Antigravity they are "agents", in VS Code they are "modes", in Codex they are "task templates").
  4. MCP Connectors → native integrations. If the rival platform also supports MCP, copy-paste. If not, you map it to its own plugin system.
  5. Scheduled tasks → orchestration. You transfer them to its cron system, GitHub Actions, n8n, or whatever the chosen platform uses.
  6. Cowork Projects → its equivalent. A Project becomes a workspace, an agent space, or whatever they call the container with custom instructions.

Sector V · Maintenance delivers Digital Sovereignty

When you can migrate, you have achieved Digital Sovereignty: the ability to create custom, dedicated work environments, with all your context, on any available agentic tool. Your CLAUDE.md, your voice principles, your glossary, your five-level structure are yours · not the provider's. When Anthropic raises prices, deprecates features, or changes terms, you move without losing your system.

Realistic adoption time

Floor 12 takes one to two Qs after consolidating floor 10 (orchestrator station with several active skills and plugins). Before that, it makes no sense to migrate because there is no robust system worth reproducing. The final test: explaining your setup to a colleague who only knows Antigravity and watching them reproduce 80% of it in a week.

Main anti-pattern

Migrating prematurely · switching tools every time a competitor releases something new. The mental rule: stay in Cowork until YOUR context is mature · only then try porting your setup to another tool. Novelty is the enemy of a living Jarvis.
Appendix · Curated Documents

★The 6 documents that make your Jarvis personal · 4 real hours

Beyond the canonical files of Sector I (CLAUDE.md root · MEMORY.md root), six curated pieces live in 00_Recursos/ and separate a generic Jarvis from one that is truly yours. Three are about how you express yourself (voice · aesthetics · biography). Three are about who you are in your relationships (legal identity · professional contacts · personal contacts). Cowork reads them in a cascade when producing each output · that cascade is called rule stacking and is the system's compound lever.

Realistic initial setup time · 4 honest hours. The automated build with prompts takes 25 minutes. The remaining 3.5 hours are spent finding contacts scattered across 3 apps, deciding which preference goes into the root CLAUDE.md vs. the voice-principles, curating between 30 relevant contacts and the 300 inflated ones. Cowork does what can be automated; your head decides what requires human judgment.

The 3 pieces on how you express yourself

brand-voice.md · how I write

Patterns from your writing extracted from 30 sent emails or 5 pasted samples · typical length per recipient, greetings, closings, formality, words you use and words you avoid, favorite transitions, preferred structure. This is the piece that makes the difference between an email that sounds like generic AI and one that sounds like you. It grows on its own · every time you edit an output and ask it to "save that preference," a new rule is added to the file. Typical mature size · 150 lines.

Before/after. Without voice-principles, Cowork drafts "Dear Andrés Felipe, I hope this email finds you well...". With voice-principles, it drafts "Hi Andrés, to give you some context..." because it knows your default greeting to trusted clients is informal and direct.

estetica.md · personal branding guidelines

Your documented design system · color palette with usage by role (primary for CTAs, accent for highlights, neutral for body), typographic stack (display + body + notes), rules for when to use bar vs. line vs. donut charts, preferred icons with their stroke-width, spacing scale, explicitly declared anti-patterns ("never use emojis in corporate docs · maximum 3 colors on a page"). Cowork reads it when generating HTML, PPTX, charts, infographics, and PDFs · the result · cross-format visual consistency without you having to impose it every time.

Before/after. Without brand-aesthetics, every deck you generate comes out with different typography and colors. With brand-aesthetics, every deck is born with your canonical palette and typefaces · the brand becomes invisibly present.

hoja-de-vida.md · source professional profile

The single source of biographical truth · education, experience with metrics (not adjectives), certifications with expiration dates, languages with CEFR level, technical skills with latest demonstrable application, soft skills with evidence, publications, public links. Cowork uses it to draft bios in three lengths (60 / 180 / 500 words), apply for opportunities, fill out profiles, and keep your LinkedIn consistent with your personal website. The non-negotiable rule · it does NOT invent achievements · if a metric is missing, it marks [PENDIENTE_DATO] and notifies you.

The 3 pieces on who you are in your relationships

identidad.md · identification data

Personal data that Cowork needs to fill out forms, draft formal letters, generate invoices, write contracts, and create your email signature · legal name, pronoun, date and city of birth, identity document redacted to a token (last 4 digits), tax information (NIT/RUC/RFC), VAT regime, postal address, public contact info. Privacy-first · it lives in local plain text with an explicit scope-rule not to be sent to an external connector without human confirmation.

contacts-pro.md · curated professional address book

Top 30 professional contacts · active clients, mentors, close team members, critical vendors, industry peers. For each one · preferred channel, tone stacked by relationship, context, last relevant interaction, expected frequency. The operating rule is fewer but relevant · 30 curated contacts > 300 imported without curation. When Cowork drafts an email to a client, it reads this file and applies the correct tone · executive client with BLUF, mentor with an open question, peer with respectful closeness · without you having to declare it each time.

Before/after. Without contacts-pro, you draft emails in "neutral mode" and manually adjust the tone every time. With contacts-pro, the email comes out with the correct tone on the first try · that difference is 5-10 minutes per important email · 3-4 hours per week.

contacts-personal.md · sensitive personal address book

Immediate family, top 10 close friends, doctors, recurring services (notary, personal accountant, mechanic, landlord), annual birthday and anniversary reminders. Sensitive information that lives in local plain text with an explicit scope-rule to NEVER be sent to a client, connector, or the cloud. Cowork respects the lock and NEVER uses this file for public outputs · only for your internal personal use (replying to your brother, preparing a birthday gift, scheduling a doctor's appointment).

Compound curation standard. These 6 documents are not built in series · they are built in concentric layers. Start with brand-voice and cv-data (2 sessions · immediate value in emails and bios). Add identity and brand-aesthetics in week 2. Finalize contacts-pro and contacts-personal in week 3. The concrete operation · SPEC prompts, templates .md verbatim and executable checklists · lives in the Runbook · Personal Jarvis OS under Steps 2 and 2a-2e.

Appendix · 8 system stations

★The 8 system stations

Sector II of the Personal Jarvis OS has 8 stations that come with the system. They cover the operating surfaces of the 21st century professional · communication, production, management, finance, development, knowledge, career and public voice. Each one answers a unique question to avoid redundancy across stations. You activate them by current pain · there is no mandatory order.

The 3 covering daily operations

Mail drafts emails with stacked tone by recipient · executive clients with BLUF, mentors with open question, peers with respectful closeness. Documents produces briefs, proposals, memos, one-pagers · each type with canonical structure. Deliveries manages recurring management artifacts · status reports, meeting notes, ADRs, risk register, retrospectives.

The 5 that lever the modern professional

Personal Finance is the full management of your money · monthly budget, categorized spending, yearly taxes, recurring subscriptions, 12-month projections. Universal because every professional needs financial order. Performance & Growth is the meta layer of your career · received and given 1:1s, formal reviews, quarterly personal OKRs, 1-3 year career plan, certifications with validity, curated reading list, skills tracker. Universal because the development curve never stops.

Study is the operation of learning as continuous habit · real learning sessions, reading notes, flashcards, paper syntheses, practical exercises, course sprints, reusable snippets. Distinct from Lab · Study is continuous and produces knowledge assets; Lab is bounded exploration with falsifiable hypothesis. Job Search is the discipline of being career-ready even without looking · master CV with its 3 lengths, ATS-friendly versions by industry, cover-letter templates, application log when applicable, pipeline of incoming opportunities, sector market intel. Publications & Social Media is the operation of public personal brand · LinkedIn posts, X threads, talks, podcasts, personal web, banners, events. Distinct from the canonical resource 00_Recursos/marca-personal/ that declares the manual · this station is the production.

Study vs Lab boundary · critical documentation. Study is expanding knowledge as continuous, structured habit. Lab is bounded exploration with falsifiable hypothesis · also covers loose tasks that are not project, cadence or study (PoCs, thought exercises, idea validations). Study can lead to opening a Lab · Lab does not become Study.

Auto-extensible memory system

The memory/ folder is the persisted context that grows without your manual effort. Five sub-folders · personas/ with one file per contact split between professionals and personals (privacy-locked), proyectos/ with identity cards for each project, decisiones/ with cross-project ADRs for longitudinal traceability, aprendizajes/ with four categories (technical, methodological, human, personal) filled at each Daily Close, and contexto/ with three live files about your current company, your industry and your personal circumstances. Cowork writes automatically in these folders at the close of each productive session · that is the memory that remembers and extends itself.

Cadences · DBR-ABR pyramid

The six canonical rhythms of Sector V form an executive pyramid with six time horizons. DBR · Daily Business Review · 10 minutes each working morning. WBR · Weekly Business Review · 45 minutes on Fridays. MBR · Monthly Business Review · 60 minutes the last Friday of each month. QBR · Quarterly Business Review · 90 minutes at each Q close. ABR · Annual Business Review · 120 minutes in December, optional for senior-level professionals planning annually. Monthly Audit · 15 minutes with a 6-question rubric (transversal to the 5 rhythms · checks system health). Each rhythm produces a traceable artifact in its corresponding folder · 04_Cadencias/{planes,repasos-semanales,repasos-mensuales,repasos-trimestrales,repasos-anuales,auditorias}/.

The canonical contract lives separately. The full taxonomy (exhaustive tree, naming conventions, privacy rules per band, Study vs Lab boundary, memory system, cadences) lives in _ESTRUCTURA.md at the root of your workspace. It is the single source of structural truth · any change happens there first. The governing HTMLs (this playbook and the runbook) regenerate from that contract.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need to pay anything to build my Jarvis?

No. The full structure (CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, sectors, stations, cadences) can be built with Claude on the free tier. Cowork Pro unlocks local filesystem + MCP connectors, which accelerates but is not essential. The method rests on plain text and flat files · always yours.

How many real hours do I need to have a living Jarvis?

Initial setup · 4 honest hours (25 min running prompts + 3.5 h organizing and curating your scattered information). To make it a habit · 1 quarter of DBR/WBR/QBR cadences. For mastery · 1+ year of disciplined use.

What if Anthropic raises prices or shuts down Claude?

Your Jarvis lives as flat files on your disk. CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, voice.md, the entire canonical tree is text. You open it in any AI IDE with filesystem access (Antigravity, Cursor, Codex, whatever comes) and it keeps working. That is Digital Sovereignty · your context travels with you, you do not migrate the product.

What is the difference between Study and Lab?

Study is expanding knowledge as continuous habit · courses, readings, syntheses, flashcards. Lab is bounded exploration with falsifiable hypothesis and final decision · also covers loose tasks (PoCs, thought exercises, validations). Study can lead to opening a Lab · Lab does not become Study.

Do I have to activate all 8 stations from day one?

No. All 8 come with the system, but you decide which to activate based on current pain. The anti-demo-only Jarvis rule · start with 2-3 stations (typically Mail + Documents + Deliveries) and add the rest when concrete pain demands them. Better a small living Jarvis than a large unused one.

How do I protect myself when pasting sensitive information?

Three privacy bands declared in root CLAUDE.md · privacy-locked (personal info, personal contacts, circumstances) which never leave to external connector · free (personal brand, professional context) Cowork reads without restriction · case-by-case confirmation (client projects, active job search). And always · validate with Compliance/Legal before uploading corporate info.

"Dare to dream big."

Now you have the method, the 5 sectors, the 8 stations and the DBR-ABR pyramid. Your next cadence is 4 hours away. And if that cadence becomes habit, the path to mastery is already charted.

AI empowers those who, with method, are capable of materializing their vision.

Javier Montaño · Co-founder · Chief Empowerment Officer · MetodologIA

Turn this playbook into a habit

If it is not scheduled, it does not exist.

Schedule the slot, run the guided prompt in Cowork and, once it works, turn the cadence into a supervised scheduled task. Calendar, prompt and AI facilitator are stackable layers; not mutually exclusive options.

DBR · Daily Review10 min · Mon-Fri · 16 weeks
Open in Google Calendar
WBR · Weekly Review45 min · Friday · 16 weeks
Open in Google Calendar
QBR · Quarterly Review90 min · Q close · one session
Open in Google Calendar
NotebookLM Google Drive Self-schedule orientation

Cadence > intensity · habit > inspiration · MetodologIA sessions are 1 hour

Did someone share this playbook with you?

You are in the right place. Take the first step.

MetodologIA is a 16-week program to (r)evolve the way you work. Method First, (Gen)AI Next. No prior experience needed · just willingness to practice.

Sign up for the workshopNo cost · Limited spots · Every month
Explore AI assistantsNo sign-up · No cost
Create Google Calendar eventAdjust time and save

Workshops every month, weeks 3 and 4. Two tracks: Best Practices or Agentic Work.

APPENDIX · POST-HARDENING 2026-05-11

What the playbook promised and the system already delivers

The playbook describes the mental model of the Jarvis OS (what/for what/why). After the 2026-05-11 hardening, the operating system is materialized. This section records the fulfilled promises and the new operational pieces that the playbook implies.

3 governance pieces per level

CLAUDE.md (rules) · MEMORY.md (history) · _INDICE.md (local map). Each canonical workspace—root, sector, station, project—has all three.

Actual state: 49 _INDICE.md · 10/10 sectors with governance · 4/4 canonical projects.

7 operative declarations (D-01 to D-07)

Operative contract encoded in CLAUDE.md root §7: TZ · language · PII · employer boundary · NotebookLM gate · canonical currency · auto-MEMORY.

Full detail in the runbook · Annex 2026-05-11 · Block 1.

Job Transition Workflow

Reusable pattern to manage a dignified job departure: dedicated project · letter + record · D-04 boundary · NotebookLM gate · post-closure checklist · final ADR.

Author's applied case (runbook annex): transition from previous employer to own company. See Block 3 · Annex in the runbook for the real detail · you adapt the pattern to your own transition.

Privacy-locked workflow

Sensitive PII lives in 00_Recursos/informacion-personal/ and memory/personas/personales/. External outputs use placeholders. Exceptions are registered as ADRs.

Privacy-locked folders never go out to external connectors.

Promise fulfilled. The playbook describes the model, the runbook describes the steps, and now the repo delivers the operating system. The three pieces live in harmony: when the playbook talks about "stations," there are 8 folders with their CLAUDE.md. When it talks about "self-extending memory," there is memory/ with _INDICE + 5 populated subfolders. When it talks about "privacy," the two locked folders with Javier's real PII exist.

Hardening verified: 62/62 checks pass · 0 legacy references · interop structure between sectors and projects · 6 cadences ready for adoption.

Visual signature · permanent triad

                    EMPRENDEDOR
                        ◇
                       / \
              HABILITADOR — ARTESANO

Entrepreneur · I risk, I found, I share · Enabler (not a consultant) · I build autonomy in others, not dependency · Artisan · I care for the craft · authority comes from the hours, not the diploma.

This triad (Entrepreneur · Enabler · Artisan) is that of the playbook's author · it closes every profile, letter, and bio he signs with his name · it functions as an identity clue for the system. When you build your Jarvis, define your own triad with words that describe your way of moving in the craft · that will be your Jarvis's signature.

MetodologIA Playbook · Personal Jarvis OS

El método para tu oficina digitalThe method for your digital office

Construye tu JarvisBuild your Jarvis

Convierte Claude Cowork en una oficina de trabajo del conocimiento que recuerda tu contexto, trabaja sobre tus archivos y te ayuda a decidir mejor. Este playbook te muestra qué construir, por qué importa, cuándo conviene hacerlo y cómo empezar sin depender de una herramienta para siempre. Metodo primero, (Gen)AI Después.

Construye una primera versión útil antes de intentar automatizar. Aprenderás a ordenar tu contexto en cinco sectores, elegir las primeras estaciones que sí sirven a tu trabajo y usar cadencias para que el sistema siga ayudando después de la primera sesión. El objetivo es simple: menos reexplicación, más evidencia y mejores decisiones.

Tu trabajo no es escribir prompts mejores conversación por conversación; es construir tu Jarvis una vez y mantenerlo vivo.
Tesis MetodologIA · Playbook · Personal Jarvis OS
Carta MetodologIA · al lector

Si esto te importa, empieza por el problema real

Llegaste porque la IA puede responder, pero tu trabajo sigue dependiendo de contexto: archivos, decisiones, compromisos, historia, expectativas del rol y criterio. Si ese contexto queda disperso, cada conversación empieza desde cero. Este playbook te ayuda a dejar de reiniciar.

Cambia el lente. Claude Cowork puede convertirse en un workspace persistente con ocho capacidades core (la octava, Dispatch, en beta · §6.8), pero el producto no es el sistema. El sistema aparece cuando tus reglas, archivos, memoria, proyectos y cadencias vuelven tu contexto portable y reutilizable.

Lo que sigue es una guía de decisión. Vas a entender qué construir, qué omitir, qué probar manualmente primero y cuándo una tarea recurrente empieza a tener sentido. Soberanía Digital significa que tu contexto viaja contigo, no que un proveedor se vuelva irremplazable.

Este playbook es Copyleft porque el conocimiento reutilizable debe viajar. Adáptalo a tu rol, tus restricciones y tus herramientas. Conserva lo que reduce fricción y descarta lo que no se gana su lugar en tu trabajo.

Metodo primero, (Gen)AI Después. Habilitadores de disrupción. Soberanía digital para ti y para los tuyos.

Abre el §1 y elige la primera decisión concreta que necesitas tomar.

Javier Andrés Montaño Guzmán Co-founder MetodologIA · Chief Empowerment Officer · Creador del Método · en nombre del equipo Medellín · MetodologIA

Visión generalLo que vas a construir

Conclusión

Tu trabajo del conocimiento se gana cocreando un sistema, no escribiendo prompts mejores. Este playbook te entrega el método para diseñar tu Jarvis sobre Claude Cowork con las ocho capacidades core, la estructura de cinco sectores operativos y un modelo mental triangulado contra fuentes verificables. Construyes una vez, mantienes vivo, (r)evolucionas con cadencia.

  • P1(R)Evolución
  • P2Intención antes que intensidad
  • P3Tecnología como aliada
Brecha · Método · Ritual

Entre probar prompts y operar un workspace confiable, la diferencia es el contexto. Este playbook muestra los hábitos, archivos y decisiones que hacen que Claude sirva al trabajo del conocimiento real. Se apoya en conocimiento público sobre Claude Cowork [13][14], en el paradigma complementario de Hyperspecific Apps y plugins [15], y en el modelo práctico de tokens, contexto y memoria.

Manifiesto MetodologIA · marco rector

Cocrear entornos de abundancia al democratizar soberanía digital y profesional.

Metodo primero, (Gen)AI Después. Habilitadores de disrupción.

Movimiento I de V I

Fundamento

Metodo primero, (Gen)AI Después.

Cubre: §1–§5 Lectura: ~50 min Pilar activo: P1 (R)Evolución
Mov. I · Fundamento · §1 / 23

§1Resumen ejecutivo

Qué es este playbook. Es un fundamento práctico para convertir Claude Cowork en un workspace que conserva contexto entre sesiones. Verás las ocho capacidades del producto, los cinco sectores que ordenan tu trabajo, las estaciones que mapean necesidades recurrentes y las cadencias que mantienen vivo el sistema. La idea no es admirar la arquitectura; es saber qué te ayuda a decidir mejor con menos fricción.

Trayectoria · cuatro fases · 4h × semana sostenidas. Manual operativo que se aplica con Claude abierto. Fase 1 (4h): lectura activa con ejercicios. Fase 2 (4-8h): setting base instalado. Fase 3 (12 semanas · 48h totales): pulido hasta competencia plena. Fase 4: operación viva con auditoría mensual y QBR trimestral.

Qué valor genera. Tres ganancias prácticas: tus archivos pueden estar disponibles sin volver a pegarlos, la memoria puede reducir explicaciones repetidas y las cadencias supervisadas pueden preparar triage, revisiones y decisiones antes de una reunión. El beneficio no es automatización mágica; es un traspaso más limpio entre tu contexto y la IA.

Idea central: el trabajo del conocimiento mejora cuando tu contexto está organizado, es portable y se puede reutilizar sin fricción. Empieza por §3 para elegir el nivel más simple que resuelve el problema de hoy, y usa §15 si necesitas una ruta corta de adopción.

Nomenclatura. Claude Desktop = app hub en tu PC con tres modos: Chat (conversacional), Cowork (knowledge work sostenido · archivos · memoria · MCP · projects · scheduled tasks) y Code (agéntico técnico). "Vives en Cowork" = modo Cowork dentro de Claude Desktop.

Sobre planes y licenciamiento (mayo 2026 · sujeto a cambios). Claude Desktop se descarga gratis en macOS y Windows. La experiencia gratuita sirve para aprender la estructura y montar los primeros archivos en texto plano. Cowork, Code, las superficies de Office, la Chrome extension, Projects, memoria persistente, scheduled tasks y conectores MCP dependen del plan y de la disponibilidad vigente. Este playbook recomienda Cowork desde la experiencia cuando estás listo para tratarlo como infraestructura de conocimiento. La decisión de costo/beneficio sigue siendo tuya: mejora cuando tienes un flujo real, contexto reutilizable y disciplina suficiente para mantener el sistema. Verifica precios y términos vigentes en claude.com/pricing antes de comprometer presupuesto.

Aceleradores opcionales mientras lees. Puedes leer este playbook solo, con tu propio método de estudio, o apoyarte en asistentes. Los asistentes no reemplazan el método: te ayudan a convertir una sección densa en preguntas de autoestudio, un plan de investigación o un prompt más limpio para tu contexto. Ábrelos en otra pestaña, pega la sección que estás estudiando y pide apoyo sin compartir datos privados.

GPT de Estudio

Úsalo cuando una sección esté densa. Pídele convertirla en preguntas de recuperación, ejercicios prácticos, flashcards y un plan corto de autoestudio.

Abrir GPT de Estudio
GPT Research Blueprint

Úsalo cuando el playbook te abra un tema que quieres investigar: MCP, memoria, gobierno, skills, scheduled tasks o soberanía digital.

Abrir GPT Research
GPT de Prompting

Úsalo cuando quieras adaptar un prompt del playbook a tu rol, proyecto, cadencia o estación sin perder estructura.

Abrir GPT de Prompting
Pristino GPT

Úsalo cuando necesites un compañero general de pensamiento para decidir cómo leer, qué priorizar o qué convertir primero en práctica.

Abrir Pristino
Notebook de fuentes y catálogos

Usa NotebookLM cuando quieras estudiar con tus propias fuentes y citas trazables. Usa los catálogos si prefieres escoger otro asistente.

Abrir NotebookLMCatálogo GPTCatálogo Gemini
Mov. I · Fundamento · §1b / 23

§1b¿Por dónde empiezo según mi rol?

El Jarvis OS es la misma arquitectura para todos · lo que cambia es la secuencia de adopción. Cinco perfiles de lector con dolor distinto · prioridad inicial distinta · primer sector a sembrar distinto. Si tu rol no encaja exactamente · escoge el más cercano y ajusta.

Tu rolDolor principalPrimer sector a sembrarPlan recomendado
Persona que trabaja con información en empresa Saturación de correos · reuniones sin acta · contextos que se pierden entre apps Sector I (identidad mínima) + Sector II Correos + Sector II Entregas (actas) Plan Express 4 semanas · output visible en 2 semanas
Líder de equipo Decisiones que se pierden · status reports manuales · feedback sin sistema Sector I + Sector II Entregas (ADRs + status) + Sector V Cadencias (WBR primero) Plan Pleno 12 semanas · adopción 1 ritual/mes
Consultor independiente / freelance Mezclar trabajos de varios clientes · perder horas en setup · brand voice inconsistente Sector I (voz + brandbook) + Sector III (un proyecto por cliente) + Sector II Documentos Plan Pleno 12 semanas · prioridad voz + plantillas comerciales
Académico / estudiante Notas dispersas · papers sin sintetizar · falta de transferencia a aplicación Sector I + Sector II Estudio (biblioteca) + Sector IV Lab (hipótesis falseables) Plan Lento 3 meses · cadencia investigador (1 sector/mes)
Creador de contenido / personal brand Inconsistencia de voz · falta de ritmo · "no sé qué publicar hoy" Sector I (voz + brandbook completo) + Sector II Publicaciones (cadencia 1/sem) Plan Express 4 semanas · primera pieza al día 14

Cualquier rol arranca por Sector I Cimientos · no se puede saltar. Lo que cambia es qué Sector II sembrar primero y qué cadencia adoptar. Si en 2 semanas tu primer sector no produjo un output que vale la pena guardar · revisa la elección antes de seguir.

Mov. I · Fundamento · §2 / 23

§2El ecosistema de superficies · dónde vive Claude en tu día

Anthropic distribuye Claude vía un hub de escritorio (Claude Desktop) que aloja tres modos (Chat, Cowork, Code) más superficies satélite (web, móvil, Office, Chrome). Este playbook vive en Cowork dentro de Claude Desktop · el corazón del knowledge work sostenido.

Aviso · wireframes ilustrativos Los esquemas y layouts que aparecen en este playbook son wireframes ilustrativos hechos en mayo de 2026, basados en capturas reales del producto al momento. Anthropic actualiza Claude Desktop con frecuencia; cuando leas este playbook en un futuro lejano, el orden exacto de los íconos, los nombres de las pestañas y la disposición de los paneles pueden haber cambiado. La intención de los wireframes no es ser fotografía fiel sino mapa mental: que puedas ubicar dónde vive cada modo y qué tipo de cosas vas a encontrar al abrir el producto. Para la versión vigente exacta, abre Claude Desktop o consultá la documentación oficial en docs.claude.com.

2.1 · Claude Desktop · el hub

Claude Desktop es la app que descargas e instalas en macOS o Windows. Aloja los tres modos bajo un mismo techo como un IDE para trabajo del conocimiento: Chat (conversacional sin archivos locales), Cowork (sostenido con archivos · memoria · MCP · skills · projects · scheduled tasks) y Code (agéntico técnico). El instalador permite aprender el método; las superficies avanzadas dependen del plan y de la disponibilidad vigente (ver callout de licenciamiento en §1). El playbook asume Cowork salvo aviso.

2.2 · Modo Chat · conversacional + Chat Projects

El Chat es interfaz conversacional pura. Disponible en Claude Desktop, Claude.ai (web), iOS y Android. Óptimo para preguntas únicas, exploración rápida, validación, redacción puntual o pensar en voz alta. No accede a archivos locales. Las apps móviles suman voz manos libres.

Dentro de Chat existen los Chat Projects: contenedores temáticos en la nube con instrucciones propias y un puñado de archivos de referencia. La promesa es directa · un asistente bien pulido finalmente te libera tiempo. Lo que antes tomaba un rato y varios prompts iterando hasta llegar al output que querías, ahora se resuelve en una sola interacción, con calidad asegurada por las custom instructions que dejaste pulidas la primera vez. Cada asistente que construyas y madures es una hora invertida hoy que te devuelve horas cada semana después · esa es la palanca operativa real, no la cantidad de asistentes que llegues a tener. Usos ideales del patrón — asistente especializado (coach, copy editor, lector crítico, según rol) · generador con formato estándar (status report, acta, one-pager) · tareas recurrentes de baja complejidad que no piden archivos locales ni memoria entre sesiones. La diferencia entre tener un prompt mental que recitas mal cada vez vs un asistente pulido que ejecuta parejo es el día y la noche de tu productividad cognitiva · empieza por uno, valídalo dos semanas, y solo entonces suma el siguiente cuando un dolor concreto lo pida.

2.3 · Modo Cowork · donde vive tu Jarvis

Cowork suma a Chat ocho capacidades core (§6): archivos locales, memoria persistente, conectores MCP, skills, Cowork Projects, browser extension, scheduled tasks [25] y Dispatch (en beta · §6.8) que extiende la oficina al móvil [23]. Es la herramienta correcta para casi todo el knowledge work recurrente con artefactos persistentes y memoria entre sesiones.

Chat Projects vs Cowork Projects. No es prestigio · es costo y profundidad. Chat Projects: baratos en tokens, ~20 archivos de referencia tope, sin filesystem. Cowork Projects: más costosos pero ilimitados — leen tu carpeta entera, recuerdan vía MEMORY.md, invocan MCP, corren scheduled tasks, producen artefactos en disco.

TareaChat ProjectCowork Project
Diseño de asistente especializado (coach, editor, lector crítico)✅ Ideal · barato · suficiente con custom instructionsSobrekill · gastas contexto que no necesitas
Generador de documento con formato estándar a partir de inputs (status, acta, one-pager)✅ Ideal · subir 5-10 archivos de referencia + plantilla en instructionsSolo si el documento exige consultar tu filesystem real
Pregunta única o exploración rápida✅ Suficiente · sin abrir ProjectInnecesario
Trabajo recurrente con cliente que produce artefactos persistentes y requiere históricoInsuficiente · pierdes memoria entre sesiones✅ Ideal · MEMORY.md acumula aprendizaje
Análisis de archivos locales pesados (CSV, PDFs largos, carpetas de cliente)No accede a tu filesystem✅ Ideal · única opción real
Automatización con scheduled tasks (clasificación matinal, draft pre-status)No las soporta✅ Única opción
Co-creación con Claude en tarea acotada (revisar texto, brainstormear título, ajustar tono)✅ Más barato y suficienteSobrekill
Plantilla corporativa codificada como skillSkills no viven en Chat✅ Skills solo en Cowork

La regla mental de bolsillo: si la tarea es recurrente, multi-archivo y exige memoria entre sesiones, Cowork. Si es acotada, autosuficiente y se beneficia de una plantilla declarada en instructions, Chat Project. Si es una pregunta única, Chat directo sin Project.

2.4 · Modo Code · agéntico técnico

Code es Claude para programación: construye, depura y despliega desde terminal o IDE. Disponible en CLI, VS Code, JetBrains, Slack. Si tienes equipo de desarrollo, conocerlo te permite calibrar tiempos con realismo. Sin supervisión técnica directa, queda fuera del alcance de este playbook.

2.5 · Microsoft Office · Claude vive dentro de tus apps

No se trata de "integraciones" externas. Se trata de que Claude está dentro de Excel, dentro de PowerPoint, dentro de Word — ya instalado, listo para operar sin que salgas de la app que tienes abierta. Cuando estás en una hoja de cálculo, Claude está ahí mismo: te ayuda con fórmulas, análisis, gráficos. Cuando estás armando una presentación, Claude está ahí: redacta slides, genera estructura, edita contenido. Cuando estás escribiendo un documento largo, Claude está ahí: te acompaña la redacción turno a turno, reformula, revisa, sugiere. La diferencia con copiar-y-pegar entre Cowork y Office es que no hay traducción: la IA opera sobre el archivo nativo, ve la celda exacta, la slide exacta, el párrafo exacto. Para roles de análisis financiero, esa misma residencia se extiende a fuentes especializadas (Daloopa, S&P Global, Moody's, LSEG, FactSet, PitchBook) accesibles desde el panel sin abrir otra pestaña.

2.6 · Chrome extension · satélite agéntico

Lleva a Claude dentro del navegador para navegar, clickear y completar formularios bajo supervisión. Útil para portales sin API, formularios regulatorios, dashboards internos. Hoy lenta y con fallos a media tarea (§6 cap 06). Limitala a exploratorio hasta que madure.

2.7 · Cómo elegir la superficie

Regla mental: pregunta dónde estás y qué haces. Movilidad → Chat móvil. Office abierto → Claude dentro de la app. Portal web → Chrome ext. Programando → Code. Resto del knowledge work → Cowork en Desktop. No excluyente: en un día tocas tres o cuatro. Forzar la superficie equivocada cuesta caro.

2.8 · Galería visual · Claude en cada superficie

Seis lugares donde Claude vive. Mismo modelo, distinta encarnación según el contexto del momento. Los wireframes son ilustrativos · mayo 2026 · el producto evoluciona, la lógica de superficie permanece.

La galería deja explícito por qué la regla de elección importa. Web y móvil son las recepciones más rápidas — entras, preguntas, sales. Excel, PowerPoint y Word alojan a Claude como panel residente que ve la celda, la slide y el párrafo exactos. La Chrome extension da capacidad agéntica sobre cualquier página web sin pedir API. Code opera desde terminal o IDE para tareas técnicas. Y por encima de todas, Cowork dentro de Claude Desktop es el hub donde tu Jarvis vive — el único lugar donde se cruzan archivos locales, memoria persistente, MCP, skills, projects y scheduled tasks.

Mov. I · Fundamento · §5b / 23

§5bCuándo NO construir un Jarvis

Construir un Jarvis personal no es para todo el mundo ni para todo momento. Hay cinco escenarios donde el método dice no empieces ahora · y reconocerlos antes ahorra meses de fricción sin retorno.

EscenarioPor qué NO ahoraQué hacer en su lugar
Tu trabajo es 100% manual sin componente digital El Jarvis es sistema operativo de trabajo del conocimiento. Sin componente textual / decisional / cross-tool · el ROI es nulo. Construye sistemas no digitales (cuadernos · rituales analógicos · tableros físicos). Vuelve al Jarvis si tu rol evoluciona hacia knowledge work.
Tu privacidad no permite contexto persistente Roles regulados (legal · salud · defensa) donde contexto persistente fuera del entorno corporativo viola compliance. Espera versión corporativa autorizada · o Jarvis solo para vida personal estrictamente separada del trabajo.
Tu organización lo prohíbe explícitamente Política de seguridad corporativa que bloquea conectores externos · agentes con acceso al filesystem · IA generativa. Respeta la política. Construye un Jarvis personal para uso fuera del trabajo (vida · proyectos paralelos · post-empleo).
No tienes 4h/semana sostenibles para 12 semanas El Jarvis se construye en un trimestre. Si tu agenda no permite esa cadencia · arrancarlo y abandonarlo a la mitad cuesta más que no empezar. Resuelve primero la causa raíz de la saturación (delegar · negar · simplificar agenda) · luego vuelve al Jarvis.
Estás en crisis aguda (laboral · personal · salud) Construir sistemas requiere bandwidth cognitivo · una crisis aguda lo consume completo. Sistema fallido en crisis = doble derrota. Atiende la crisis · estabiliza · luego construye el Jarvis con la lucidez recuperada (3-6 meses post-estabilización).

Si ninguno de los cinco escenarios aplica a tu situación · el Jarvis es viable. Si uno aplica parcialmente · ajusta el plan (más lento · scope más pequeño · solo vida personal · etc.) antes de invertir las 4 horas de la primera sesión.

El método es el catalizador. La IA operada con método te lleva más lejos, más rápido, con más precisión, calidad y escala. Sin método, la mejor IA del mundo se vuelve otra pestaña abierta más.
Tesis del Movimiento I · efecto catalizador
Mov. I · Fundamento · §3 / 23

§3Los doce niveles de adopción · del uso a la soberanía

Cowork no es la única opción. Vale la pena declarar que el mapa de adopción que este playbook describe no requiere específicamente Claude Cowork. El mismo diseño conceptual se puede armar con otras herramientas agénticas del mercado: Antigravity de Google, Visual Studio Code de Microsoft con Copilot, Codex de OpenAI. Cowork no es mejor por ser único; es mejor por la fricción operativa que remueve cuando trabajas con archivos locales, memoria persistente y conectores MCP de forma cotidiana. Si tu dolor principal es otro, la elección razonable puede ser otra herramienta.

Piensa en las herramientas como niveles de trabajo. Cada nivel suma capacidad y también suma complejidad. La regla es simple: usa la superficie más simple que resuelva bien la tarea y sube solo cuando el trabajo lo exija. Agregar complejidad antes de tiempo consume energía; quedarte en una superficie simple cuando necesitas más contexto, archivos o automatización produce frustración y mal output. Esta sección te muestra los doce niveles en tres movimientos: primero resuelves con lo disponible, después trabajas con tus archivos del computador y, al final, construyes componentes propios cuando una fricción se vuelve recurrente.

El error más común al elegir herramienta es ir por costumbre o por la pestaña que tenías abierta, no por criterio. Eso te hace gastar una hora en Chat directo haciendo algo que un Chat Project hubiera resuelto en cinco minutos sin instalar nada, o intentar análisis profundo de archivos del cliente desde el móvil cuando Cowork directo en escritorio te lo hubiera resuelto en una sesión sostenida. El mapa de niveles convierte esa decisión en algo replicable porque te hace preguntarte siempre lo mismo: cuál es la superficie más simple que esta tarea exige.

3.1 · Cinco sectores horizontales · dos ejes que se cruzan

El mapa de niveles del Jarvis tiene dos ejes que se mueven en direcciones distintas y complementarias. El eje vertical es la profundidad de adopción: los doce niveles progresan de Chat directo hasta migrar tu setting a otra herramienta, y se aprenden uno tras otro porque cada nivel pide más capacidad operativa que el anterior. El eje horizontal son los cinco sectores, que existen todos desde el día uno y están disponibles en cualquier momento del flujo. No se desbloquean con la madurez — están abiertos siempre, listos para ser usados según lo que la tarea pida en este instante. Subir de nivel es ganar capacidad. Entrar a un sector es elegir desde qué capa de tu arquitectura operas ahora mismo.

Los doce niveles no se memorizan uno por uno. Se recuerdan mejor agrupados en cinco sectores horizontales — bandas que cruzan el mapa completo y que dan respuesta a la única pregunta que importa cuando eliges herramienta: ¿en qué sector estoy operando ahora? Puedes estar en el nivel uno usando Chat directo y al mismo tiempo tener disponible el sector V Mantenimiento porque ya tienes tu CLAUDE.md raíz capaz de migrar a otra herramienta. Los tres movimientos narrativos (resolver · trabajar · construir) describen la progresión temporal; los cinco sectores describen el mapa siempre desplegado en el que esa progresión ocurre.

Cada sector es además una capa de tu arquitectura de información. Cuando hable de "capas" me refiero al equivalente físico del sector en tu filesystem: la carpeta donde vive el contexto que Claude necesita para operar en ese sector. Sectores y capas son la misma realidad vista desde dos ángulos. El sector es la experiencia del trabajo que estás haciendo. La capa es la carpeta que sostiene esa experiencia. Las cinco capas conviven desde el momento en que armas el scaffolding raíz; lo que cambia con el tiempo no es cuántas hay disponibles, sino cuán pobladas están de contexto útil.

Sector horizontalCapa · carpeta del scaffoldingPara qué sirve
I · CimientosN0 · Root + 00_Recursos/Identidad, marca, governance — lo que no cambia
II · BaseN1 · Estaciones/Operación cotidiana — Correos, Pre-venta, Entregas
III · NúcleoN2 · Projects/Expedientes vivos — clientes, iniciativas, proyectos por código (P-NNN según tu numeración · ej: P-001, P-002, P-003)
IV · I+D+iN3 · Lab/Investigación, desarrollo e innovación — estaciones orquestadoras, mini apps, skills propias en gestación
V · MantenimientoN4 · Cadencias/Ritmo y portabilidad de TU contexto — scheduled tasks, independencia de proveedor, tu sistema (CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, voice, projects, history) viaja contigo (Soberanía Digital)

La idea fuerte: tu filesystem es la arquitectura de información de tu Jarvis. Las cinco carpetas raíz no son una convención cosmética — son cinco señales que Claude lee al iniciar cualquier sesión sobre tu workspace, y que le avisan automáticamente dónde está todo y cómo se conecta. Cuando abres Cowork sobre Trabajo en Claude Desktop/, Claude no necesita que le expliques el sistema porque el sistema está escrito en los nombres mismos. 00_Recursos/ dice "esto es identidad y referencia". 01_Estaciones/ dice "aquí vive la operación cotidiana". 02_Proyectos/ dice "aquí están los expedientes vivos". 03_Lab/ dice "aquí experimento sin romper producción". 04_Cadencias/ dice "aquí está lo que corre solo".

Eso convierte la organización del disco duro en contexto pasivo: una documentación que no escribes pero que Claude entiende. Un CLAUDE.md en la raíz lo formaliza con reglas explícitas, pero las carpetas ya hablan por sí solas. Por eso la regla de naming en kebab-case con prefijo numérico no es decisión estética — es la interfaz por la cual tu intención se vuelve legible para una IA sin que tengas que repetirla en cada conversación. Renombrar una carpeta de misc/ a 02_Proyectos/ no mueve un byte de información, pero le entrega a Claude un mapa que antes no tenía.

De aquí en adelante usaremos dos términos con disciplina. Sector cuando hablemos de la banda horizontal en la que estás operando ahora — siempre hay cinco disponibles, puedes moverte entre ellas según lo que la tarea pida. Capa cuando hablemos de la materialización del sector en tu filesystem como carpeta con su CLAUDE.md, su MEMORY.md y su contexto propio. Los cinco sectores conviven desde el día uno; lo que asciende a lo largo del tiempo es tu trabajo dentro de ellos, hasta que el sector V Mantenimiento — donde viven las cadencias automáticas y la portabilidad de TU contexto (info, proyectos, desarrollos, preferencias, historia · todo es tuyo, no del producto) — deja de ser una carpeta vacía y entrega la Soberanía Digital: la prueba de que el sistema entero ya no depende del proveedor.

3.2 · Movimiento uno · niveles de entrada

El primer movimiento resuelve desde la nube. Tres pisos en Claude Chat: lo simple, lo curado, lo focalizado. Suficiente para la mayoría de preguntas que no piden tus archivos locales.

El piso uno es Chat directo en claude.ai web o app móvil. Una pregunta, una respuesta, sin ceremonia. El punto de entrada más rápido: exploración, validación, voz mientras caminas entre reuniones.

El piso dos es Chat con prompts de alto rendimiento. Dejás de improvisar y operas con tu biblioteca personal de plantillas probadas: resumir actas, descomponer problemas, redactar email ejecutivo. MCP opcional para mirar afuera del chat.

El piso tres es Chat Projects: contenedores temáticos en la nube con instrucciones propias y archivos de referencia. Tu primer asistente especializado — pre-venta, comités, feedback — sin tocar el filesystem local.

Subes a Cowork cuando la tarea pide tu computador: archivos locales, memoria persistente vía MEMORY.md, MCP profundo, artifacts en disco.

3.3 · Movimiento dos · departamentos del Jarvis

El segundo movimiento trabaja sobre tus archivos. Cowork sostiene memoria persistente y MCP propios. Dos pisos para el trabajo del conocimiento sostenido.

El piso cuatro es Cowork directo: app de escritorio sobre una carpeta de trabajo. Sesiones únicas con archivos locales que aún no merecen Project formal — análisis ad hoc, (R)Evolución de PDFs, conversión de legacy.

El piso cinco es Cowork Projects: contenedores con instrucciones, archivos curados y memoria persistente vía MEMORY.md. Trabajo recurrente con un cliente, iniciativa o programa — cada uno con su rol, tono y memoria propia.

Subes a construir cuando la repetición pide automatización: misma plantilla tres semanas seguidas, mismo flujo de risk cada vez, misma rutina de cierre de sprint. Esa es la señal.

3.4 · Movimiento tres · sucursales propias

El tercer movimiento construye tus piezas. Cuando un patrón se repite, lo materializas como pieza reutilizable. Siete pisos en orden ascendente de sofisticación y alcance organizacional.

El piso seis son Skills: instrucciones reutilizables que Cowork carga bajo demanda. Plantilla de status report como atajo, risk entry como flujo, retrospectiva como comando. Regla de tres — si se repite tres veces, vale la pena materializar.

El piso siete son Plugins: empaquetados que combinan skills, comandos y MCP en una unidad instalable. Donde una skill sirve a uno, un plugin sirve al equipo. Tienda privada o pública con versionado.

El piso ocho son mini apps via vibe coding: describes la intención en lenguaje natural, la IA escribe el código. Hyperspecific Apps que resuelven un cuello de botella tuyo y solo tuyo.

El piso nueve es plugin engineering versionado. Cuando una mini app la usan dos o más colegas, sube a plugin oficial con CHANGELOG, semver, pruebas y revisión por pares.
